title: SHA256 via precompile (address 0x2) with mload result and invalid() on failure
explanation: staticcall to address 0x2 runs SHA256. Output is written to 0x01 (overlaps scratch space). result := mload(staticcall(...)) works because staticcall returns 1 on success — mload(1) reads from memory offset 1 which is where the 32-byte output was written. On failure returndatasize() is 0, triggering invalid() which consumes all remaining gas (stronger than revert for unrecoverable precompile failures).
when_to_apply: Any SHA256 computation — Bitcoin merkle proofs, cross-chain verification, EIP-712 with SHA256
when_not_to_apply: When keccak256 is acceptable — SHA256 precompile costs ~60 gas base vs keccak256 opcode at 30 gas base
